rulings.Case Title: DEMAKAS PLUMBLING &. HEATING, INC. VS. STRATFORD HOTEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Party Defendant "Alter-Ego"
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 11, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DEMAKAS PLUMBLING &. HEATING, INC.'s Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Party Defendant "Alter-Ego" Plaintiff Demakas Plumbing & Heating, Inc.'s motion to amend judgment to add Party Defendant "Alter-Ego/Successor" as judgment debtor is granted. No opposition filed. Dalwong Hotel Group, LLC is added as a judgment debtor as the alter-ego of Union Square Hotel Company. The Court has the authority under Code of Civil Procedure section 187 to amend a judgment to include an alter ego who was not a party to the underlying suit but nevertheless had control of the litigation as a judgment debtor. (Jack Farenbaugh & Son v. Belmont Construction, Inc. (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 1023, 1028-30.) The uncontested evidence submitted with the motion supports amending the judgment to include Dalwong as a judgment debtor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

Pursuant to plaintiff's request, the court vacates the default and default judgment and dismisses this case without prejudice. rulings.Case Title: JASON ROUSSOS VS. POUR GUYS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross Complaint By Defendant And Cross-Complainant Pour Guys Soma Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 11, 2019, Line 4, CROSS COMPLAINANT POUR GUYS SOMA LLC DBA TEMPEST BAR & GRILL's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross Complaint Defendant Pour Guys SOMA LLC's motion for leave to file first amended cross-complaint is denied. Although California's policy of liberality in amendment of pleadings generally warrants granting a request for leave to file an amended pleading before trial, under the unusual circumstances of this case the Court believes that the potential prejudice to Plaintiff Jason Roussos outweighs that policy, particularly given Defendant's lengthy delay in seeking to amend, the potential prejudice to Plaintiff, and Defendant's ability to protect its interests by presenting evidence at trial or seeking indemnity or contribution from third parties after trial. This is a premises liability action in which Plaintiff seeks to recover for personal injuries suffered in May 2016, when he was stabbed by an employee at a bar owned by Defendant. Plaintiff filed his initial complaint on March 2, 2017, and the case was originally set for trial on April 30, 2018. The trial date has been continued on four previous occasions, and is currently set for July 8, 2019. On January 31, 2019, when the Court last continued the trial date, it explicitly stated that "other than treating medical health care providers, fact discovery is closed." Defendant now seeks to amend its cross-complaint to add two new cross-defendants, Wendy O'Connor, L.M.F.T., and Brandy Cohen-Brown, D.O. Dr. O'Connor is a licensed marriage and family therapist, and has treated Plaintiff since he was injured in 2016; in addition, Plaintiff has designated her as an expert witness in this case. Dr. Cohen-Brown also has provided psychiatric care and treatment to Plaintiff since the incident. Defendants took their depositions on January 16 and 25, 2019, respectively. Defendant now claims that the medical care and treatment provided by Dr. O'Connor and Dr. Cohen-Brown was a substantial factor in exacerbating Plaintiff's personal injuries, such that it should be able to name them in cross-complaints for equitable indemnification and equitable contribution. Allowing Defendant, at this late date, to amend to bring in as new parties Plaintiff's health care professionals, one of whom Plaintiff has designated as an expert witness, would severely prejudice Plaintiff. It would place those health care professionals in a conflict of interest, would force Plaintiff to obtain a new expert witness to replace Dr. O'Connor, and would likely require Plaintiff to seek new treating therapists and thereby undermine his medical treatment and condition. Further, it inevitably would cause substantial additional delay and expense, requiring reopening discovery and a further continuance of the trial date. (See California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2018) 6:565 ["A greater showing of 'interest of justice' is requjired to obtain leave to file a cross-complaint against . . . some third person not yet a party to the action. Here, the court will be concerned that the cross-complaint not unreasonably burden and complicate plaintiff's lawsuit with cross-actions and third parties."].) Further, naming these physicians as cross-defendants is unnecessary to protect Defendant's interests. If Defendant can show, as it asserts, that they provided negligent medical treatment that aggravated Plaintiff's personal or psychological injuries, they may present such evidence at trial to reduce their exposure to noneconomic damages. (See Henry v. Superior Court (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 440.) It may also file independent actions for indemnity or contribution following trial on the underlying claims. The entirety of this tentative ruling is emailed to parties. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MINOO PARSAZADEH VS. HAMPTON HEALTH, LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Hampton Health, Ltds Verified Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 11, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MINOO PARSAZADEH'S Motion For Order Compelling Hampton Health, Ltds Verified Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff MINOO PARSAZAHEH'S Motion for Order Compelling Hampton Health Ltd.'s Verified Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set Two is DENIED. It appears from the moving papers that on March 22, 2019, defendant Hampton Health provided verified responses to plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, Set Two. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rebecca.woodson@dentons.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER ORAZIO VS. CAMBRIA COMPANY LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 11, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JENNIFER ORAZIO's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint Plaintiff Jennifer Orazio's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted on the following conditions: (1) Plaintiff shall make herself available for a reopened deposition as to the newly asserted cause of action for retaliatory discharge; (2) the discovery cut-off date is extended by 60 days to July 19; (3) Defendants shall have until August 23 to file a motion for summary adjudication as to the new cause of action, and that motion may be heard on less than 75 days' notice; and (4) the current June 24, 2019 trial date is vacated, and trial is continued to a mutually agreeable date. Counsel shall meet and confer to agree upon a new trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION VS. BODUM USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Gibson Overseas, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 11, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Gibson Overseas, Inc. Plaintiff Ecological Rights Foundation's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and for entry of consent judgment as to Defendant Gibson Overseas, Inc. is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). The Court will execute the proposed order and consent judgment included with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)
